title: Arbiter of the Grade — Aftermath
description: The Arbiter writes an A in his grade book and cannot account for how it happened. The rubric had no category for applied experience. He has been grading a class whose subject he can no longer name. The Bard's comment about the rubric being a love letter to certainty stays with him.

lines: The Arbiter received the outcome with the equanimity of a man consulting a ledger and finding a line he doesn't quite understand.
Room 204 was very quiet. The motivational eagles on the walls continued to soar above the standard without comment.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: No conditions. That was an A.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: My rubric was perfect. My curve was calibrated precisely. How did you pass without studying?

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: We studied our whole lives. Just not these subjects.

lines.speaker: Cleric
lines.text: The underlying principles are the same. Every healing decision uses triage logic. Every tactical choice is resource allocation.

steps.text: The Arbiter looked at his grade book. Something moved across his face that was not quite recognition and not quite discomfort.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: You are not the students this curriculum was designed for. And yet the curriculum worked.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I don't know whether to mark that as a success.

steps.text: He was quiet for a moment, looking at the cover of the grade book in the way of a man who has just noticed the title of a book he has been reading for years.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I don't know what class this is for. I've been grading it a long time.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I'm not sure anymore what the subject is.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I don't have a column for interesting.

steps.text: He wrote something in the margin anyway. His pen moved with the particular slowness of someone writing something they didn't expect to write.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I've never had to curve the grade before.
